title: Provider Adapter Contract
purpose: Contract for binding an LLM provider adapter to the compute provider registry, ledger rows, budget gates, fallback policy, and provider receipts before runtime provider code is refactored or added.

provenance.transfer_note: deepscroll's provider-keyed LLMInterface plus fallback-provider shape is translated here into an ai_workflow standard. Runtime refactors are intentionally deferred behind the 09.44 provider-ledger proof.

provider_id.description: Stable provider key. Must match a provider row in codex/doctrine/compute/provider_registry.json.

adapter_id.description: Stable adapter key for the provider binding. Prefer <provider_id> or <provider_id>:<api_shape> when one provider has multiple adapter shapes.

adapter_module.description: Import path or runtime binding that owns the adapter implementation, such as system.lib.nvidia_nim. Contract landing does not require this module to exist yet.

api_shape.description: Provider call shape exposed by the adapter.

base_url_policy.description: Whether callers must, may, or must not configure a base URL for this adapter.

auth_env_refs.description: Environment variable names or secret references required by the adapter. Values are never stored in this contract.

model_resolution.description: Model selection policy. explicit_model_required must be true for governed provider work; model aliases must resolve through provider registry or ledger rows.

capabilities.description: Capability flags that gate task-class routing and receipt expectations.

rate_limit_claims.description: Provider or model limit claims with explicit source posture. Unknowns must stay visible instead of becoming hidden SDK defaults.

fallback_policy.description: Named fallback is same-job recovery only. It must not collapse independent provider lanes into one global winner.

execution_limits.description: Adapter-level ceilings enforced before provider calls.

budget_policy.description: Default budget posture for this adapter. Automatic lower-class workers default to free-only unless an explicit operator budget exists.

receipt_requirements.description: Fields that must be present in provider receipts when the adapter is used for governed work.

fallback_policy.rule: Fallback chains are named, ledger-governed, budget-gated, and receipt-visible.
fallback_policy.lane_boundary: Fallback is same-job recovery after a selected lane is unavailable. It is not a scheduler rule for idling another independent provider lane.
fallback_policy.receipt_rule: Every fallback attempt must be visible in the provider receipt with attempted provider_id, model_id, status, and reason.

runtime_refactor_gate.rule: Runtime adapter code must not land from this standard alone.
required_before_runtime_refactor: At least one bounded provider_transform_job has written a validated provider_receipt and row_patch under state/compute_workers/
metabolism_scheduler broad-chat blocking remains verified for NVIDIA and OpenRouter provider lanes

p005_compatibility_appendix.rule: Future provider adapters must declare model-family API parameter compatibility before use.
p005_compatibility_appendix.non_goal: This appendix does not adopt p005 as a runtime code change in the p004 contract landing.

validation_rules: explicit_model_required must be true for governed provider work.
default_model_allowed must be false unless a registry model profile explicitly names the default and its budget posture.
fallback_policy.allowed=true requires a non-empty named chain, cooldown_seconds, and budget_gate_required=true.
rate_limit_claims.source_kind must be one of official_doc, account_probe, assumed_default, or unknown.
budget_policy must make free-only and paid-call defaults explicit.
receipt_requirements must require provider_id, model_id, request_id, rate_limit snapshot, budget snapshot, and fallback attempts.
Adapters must not hide provider SDK fallback, retry, budget, or rate-limit behavior behind unrecorded defaults.
Adapter rows may guide implementation only after runtime_refactor_gate.required_before_runtime_refactor is satisfied.
